zh.lifeSummary: 當Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegem於1300年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯雷迪時，他的父親Baudouin I de Renty20歲，母親Gertrude de Falvy20歲。他於1321年與Marie de Brimeu在法國豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯雷迪結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1369年享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegem于1300年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯雷迪时，他的父亲Baudouin I de Renty20岁，母亲Gertrude de Falvy20岁。他于1321年与Marie de Brimeu在法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯雷迪结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1369年享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Renty,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Fenton  .

variants.name: Fenton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places, in Yorkshire, Lincolnshire, Northumberland, Staffordshire, and Nottinghamshire, so called from Old English fenn ‘marsh, fen’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

Irish: English surname adopted by bearers of Gaelic Ó Fionnachta or Ó Fiachna ‘descendant of Fiachna’, an old personal name Anglicized as Feighney and sometimes mistranslated as Hunt (see Fee 1 and Finnerty ).
Scottish: habitational name from Fenton in East Lothian.

nameMeanings.name: Heer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Alfons, Alois, Ewald, Kurt, Monika, Otto.
German and Dutch: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the first element hari, heri ‘army’, as, for instance, Hermann . This surname is rare in the Netherlands.
Dutch: variant, without the definite article de ‘the’, of De Heer .

variants.name: Heerema
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Egbert.
West Frisian and Dutch (Groningen): patronymic from the personal name Heer , composed with the Frisian suffix -ma ‘(one of the) men of’.

variants.name: Heeringa
meanings: West Frisian: habitational name from any of several mansions or farms so called, for instance near Dongjum and Marssum, once inhabited by a certain Heere or Hero and his kin (see Heer 1). It is formed with the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a.

variants.name: Hehr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elfriede, Erhart, Horst, Milbert.
German: variant of Heer 1 and 3, but in some instances a nickname either from hëher ‘jay’, or from Middle High German hēr ‘distinguished, proud’.

variants.name: Heeren
meanings: Dutch, North German, and East Frisian: patronymic from   Heer   1.

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: De Heer
meanings: Dutch: from heer ‘(feudal) lord, master’, with the definite article de ‘the’, probably applied as a nickname to someone who behaved in a lordly manner, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a feudal lord or the like. Compare Heer .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Renty
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Fenton  .

variants.name: Fenton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places, in Yorkshire, Lincolnshire, Northumberland, Staffordshire, and Nottinghamshire, so called from Old English fenn ‘marsh, fen’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

Irish: English surname adopted by bearers of Gaelic Ó Fionnachta or Ó Fiachna ‘descendant of Fiachna’, an old personal name Anglicized as Feighney and sometimes mistranslated as Hunt (see Fee 1 and Finnerty ).
Scottish: habitational name from Fenton in East Lothian.

nameMeanings.name: en
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Chong, Young, Chun, Hang, Ho, In, Jung, Maeng, Mijin, Rong, Hyong, Pong, Then, Yom, Lanh, Nu.  Cambodian Chea, Chhay, Chheng, Chhom, Voeun, Yoeun.  Spanish Alejandro, Angel, Miguel, Ricardo, Secundino, Tomas.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 阮, see   Ruan   3.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 殷 and 尹, see   Yin   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ruan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Xiu, Ying, Biao, Chao, Cheng, Guang, Guohua, Hua, Huiming, Jia, Jian Hua.  Spanish Jorge, Arnulfo, Arturo, Blanca, Carlos, Eduardo, Gregorio, Guillermo, Javier, Jesus, Jose, Josefina.
Irish and Scottish: variant of   Ruane  .
Spanish (Ruán): nickname from ruán ‘roan’ (a color of horses), a variant of Ruano .

nameMeanings.name: Andreas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The original New Testament Greek form of   Andrew  , used in the English-speaking world as a learned variant.

variants.name: Andries
meanings: form of the Greek name Andreas, a short form of any of various compound names derived from andr- ‘man, warrior’. In the New Testament this is the name of the first disciple to be called by Jesus. After the Resurrection, St Andrew preached in Asia Minor and Greece. He is traditionally believed to have been crucified at Patras in Achaia. He was one of the most popular saints of the Middle Ages and was adopted as the patron of Scotland, Russia, and Greece. It has long been among the most popular boys' names in the English-speaking world, especially in Scotland. Its popularity in England was further enhanced by its use as a British royal name for Prince Andrew ( b. 1960 ), the Duke of York.

en.lifeSummary: When Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegem was born in 1300, in Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, his father, Baudouin I de Renty, was 20 and his mother, Gertrude de Falvy, was 20. He married Marie de Brimeu in 1321, in Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died in 1369, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegem est né en 1300, à Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Baudouin I de Renty, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Gertrude de Falvy, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Marie de Brimeu en 1321, à Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1369, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegem 1300 in Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Baudouin I de Renty 20 und seine Mutter Gertrude de Falvy 20. Er hat 1321 in Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich Marie de Brimeu geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb 1369 im Alter von 69 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegem è nato il nel 1300 nel Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Baudouin I de Renty, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Gertrude de Falvy, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Marie de Brimeu nel 1321, nel Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì nel 1369, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegemが1300年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスレントイで生まれた時，父Baudouin I de Rentyは20歳，母Gertrude de Falvyは20歳であった。1321年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスレントイでMarie de Brimeuと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1369年に69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegem(이)가 1300년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 파스-데-캘레이 렌티에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baudouin I de Renty(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Gertrude de Falvy(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1321년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 파스-데-캘레이 렌티에서 Marie de Brimeu(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1369년 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegem nasceu em 1300, em Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Baudouin I de Renty, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Gertrude de Falvy, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Marie de Brimeu em 1321, em Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1369, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegem родился в 1300 г. в местности ренти, пас-де-калеи, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Baudouin I de Renty было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Gertrude de Falvy, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Marie de Brimeu в 1321 г. в местности ренти, пас-де-калеи, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер в 1369 г. в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Heer Andreas de Renty, Heer van Renty en Senegem nació en 1300, en Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Baudouin I de Renty, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Gertrude de Falvy, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Marie de Brimeu en 1321, en Renty, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió en 1369, a la edad de 69 años.
